bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with their worst possible offer for me (all valueF), which gives me very low utility. Since we're at t=0.00, I should open with my ideal bid to maximize utility. My best values are valueA across all issues, giving me maximum utility of 1.0 on each issue. There's no reason to concede at this early stage.

The opponent is making very low offers (all F/E values) and has barely conceded (only issueD moved from valueF to valueE). We're very early in the negotiation (t=0.02), so there's no pressure to concede yet. I should stay close to my top-utility bid. My best bid is all valueA giving utility 1.0. I'll maintain a strong position but can slightly vary to show willingness to negotiate - I'll keep issueA at valueA (highest weight) and hold firm on most issues.

It's very early (t=0.04), and the opponent is making tiny concessions (moving from all-F to slight variations). My best bid is all-A values giving me utility of 1.0. I'll maintain my maximum utility bid since there's no pressure to concede yet, and the opponent's offers are still very low utility for me (around 0.2).

The opponent is making very slow concessions, mostly stuck at valueF/valueE across all issues, which gives me very low utility. We're only at t=0.06, so there's no time pressure yet.
